title: zanthoxylum monogynum subsp. monogynum
intro: Zanthoxylum monogynum subsp. monogynum, also known as the Korean prickly ash, is a plant species native to East Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, pain, and infections. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support digestive health, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used in traditional medicine in East Asia.
